title: AI transforms software industry with workflow continuity and ...
full_text:
Today’s Wall Street Journal features a very interesting article by Belle L. on how companies are using AI and its impact on the software industry. There is, of course, the argument that most large businesses would rather utilize AI to enhance core innovations rather than routine operations. And then there’s the argument about vibe-coding certain applications in-house on top of software to avoid increasing tech costs. These are all valid ways in which AI will change software spending. However, an important aspect that we need to talk about more is how the software industry is metamorphosing today to become the connector for AI Agents.
AI has changed software, making it more pliable for integrations and enabling workflow continuity across different tools in business categories. At Egnyte, for instance, we are the connective tissue that works within business applications to ensure unified workflows while maintaining a single source of truth. I believe that this is the future of software, especially in the data and content management side of the house. AI’s value in the core business is going to be exponential for enterprises of all sizes, and the same is true for the software industry as well. The only difference for specialized software like ERP, CRM, or Content Repository is the focus on using AI not only to build a better solution but also be able to act as the thread within the complex web of knowledge and context in an organization.
Adding the link to the article in the first comment.
